other_observers: Rachel Hopper, Bill Schmoker who took photographs, Doug Faulkner and John Vanderpoel.; First heard and identified by Joey Kellner.
duration: Viewed for 20 minutes but heard for almost two hours
specific_location: Southwest corner of Cottonwood Marsh, Walden Ponds which are off 75th Street east of Boulder.
description: A very small, but obvious member of the Heron family. Black head and eye contrasted with orangeish colored bill, face and neck. The bill was  relatively long and pointed for the size of the bird, which was about a foot long. The back was also black, and there were obvious, large creamish patches on the wings. The breast was light, and the legs were yellow. I only saw the bird moving at the base of cattails, and did not see the bird fly.Behaviors: The bird was moving slowly through the base of a quite small cattail marsh. It was probably looking for food, even though it was singing frequently.Call: The bird sang very frequently and persistently over  the two hours I was there, and was recorded by  Nathan Pieplow.Plumage: Alternate
